ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for banana cake or a café set meal. FOODON_00002473 (cake food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Cakes and pastries) is the closest parent. No specific banana cake or set-meal code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for banana cake set meal. SNOMED CT does not have a specific food concept at this level of specificity.

serving.description: 1 slice of banana cake (approx. 100 g) + 1 medium hot beverage (approx. 240 ml)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving consists of one slice of banana cake (approximately 100 g) paired with one medium-sized hot beverage such as kopi or teh (approximately 240 ml), as served at Toast Box Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Banana cake, homemade-style — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18009 analogous); hot beverage (kopi/teh with condensed milk) estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo coffee with milk entry
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'banana cake set with medium hot beverage' exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values for the banana cake component are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for banana bread/cake (NDB 18009, Banana bread, prepared from recipe). The hot beverage component (medium kopi or teh with condensed milk, ~240 ml) is estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo data for coffee with condensed milk. Combined per-100g values are weighted by component mass: banana cake ~100 g, hot beverage ~240 g, total ~340 g. All values represent the blended composite per 100 g of the full set.

gi_source.title: Banana cake, made with sugar — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 51 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for banana cake made with sugar (GI=51, low category), as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values. The hot beverage component (kopi/teh with condensed milk) contributes additional carbohydrates; the composite GL per serving is calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (51 × 58.4 g carbohydrate in 340 g serving) / 100 ≈ 29.8. The GI of 51 is applied to the composite set as the banana cake dominates the glycemic response.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and added sugar from condensed milk in hot beverage
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful that this set meal provides approximately 333 mg sodium per serving (340 g), and the condensed milk in the hot beverage adds significant sugar; limiting frequency of consumption and requesting less condensed milk can help manage sodium and sugar intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and moderate glycemic index with substantial glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this set meal as it delivers approximately 50 g of sugar per serving and a glycemic load of ~30, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting the hot beverage with unsweetened kopi-o or teh-o and limiting portion size is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and eggs in cake, and trans fat from baking
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should note that this set meal provides approximately 8 g of saturated fat per serving, primarily from butter and eggs in the banana cake; occasional consumption within a heart-healthy dietary pattern is acceptable, but regular intake should be limited in line with AHA guidelines on saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Banana Cake Set with Medium Hot Beverage is a popular café set meal offered at Toast Box, a Singaporean kopitiam-style chain. The set consists of one slice of moist, dense banana cake paired with a medium-sized hot beverage, typically kopi (Singaporean-style coffee brewed with robusta beans and sweetened condensed milk) or teh (milk tea with condensed milk). The banana cake is a baked product made from ripe bananas, wheat flour, eggs, butter, and sugar, yielding a soft, slightly sweet crumb with a pronounced banana flavour and golden-brown crust. The hot beverage contributes additional sweetness and creaminess from condensed milk. Nutritionally, the set is moderate in energy (~503 kcal per serving), relatively high in sugar (~50 g per serving) and provides moderate saturated fat (~8 g per serving). The glycemic index of the banana cake component is approximately 51 (low-medium boundary), but the glycemic load per full serving is high (~30) due to the combined carbohydrate contribution of both the cake and the sweetened beverage. This set is a common breakfast or afternoon snack choice in Singapore's kopitiam culture. Clinically, it is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease due to its sugar and saturated fat content. Sodium is moderate at approximately 333 mg per serving. The set is vegetarian and generally halal-certified at Toast Box outlets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a banana cake set from Toast Box?
What is the GI of banana cake?
Banana cake set with kopi calories
How much sugar in banana cake set?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for 'Banana Cake Set with Medium Hot Beverage' exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for banana bread/cake with HPB Singapore Foodo data for kopi/teh with condensed milk, weighted by component mass. The GI value is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for banana cake made with sugar (GI=51), which is the closest defensible analogous entry. Confidence is medium because the composite nature of the set meal and the variability in hot beverage preparation (kopi vs teh, amount of condensed milk) introduce estimation uncertainty.
notes: 1. This is a composite set meal record. Per-100g values represent the blended nutritional profile of the full set (banana cake ~100 g + hot beverage ~240 g = ~340 g total). 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy banana bread entry or the HPB Foodo coffee/tea entries. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary sources used for this composite estimate. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary sources used for this composite estimate. 5. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy banana bread data and may vary. 6. The hot beverage component is assumed to be kopi or teh with condensed milk as is standard at Toast Box; substitution with black coffee or plain tea would significantly reduce sugar, energy, and GI. 7. Halal status is based on Toast Box's general halal certification in Singapore; individual outlet certification should be verified. 8. GI of 51 is from the University of Sydney GI Database for banana cake made with sugar (Atkinson et al. 2008); this is an analogous estimate applied to the composite set. 9. All -1 sentinel values indicate nutrients not reported in the approved sources used for this record.
